objective: Close the remaining governance-source rollout blockers left after issues #583 and #585 so downstream product-repo implementation can resume safely, with sim-backed specialist agents and packet contracts hard-gated before downstream rollout resumes.
scope_boundary: Issue-backed plan, scope, handoff, review, validation, and closeout artifacts for the final rollout-blocker fixes.
Validator, schema, local-CI profile, governance tooling package, consumer pull-state, standards, and runbook changes required to clear the issue-587 blocker set.
Replay proof against downstream failure shapes without mutating downstream repos in this lane.
out_of_scope: Direct product-repo repair in Stampede or other governed consumers in this branch.
Reopening merged governance-source fixes except where required by the final audit blocker set.
Warning-only or documentation-only treatment of the blocker set.
Unrelated bootstrap, graphify, remote-MCP, or provider wiring changes.
research_summary: The final post-#585 governance-source audit, revalidated against current main and reconciled with the accepted Claude alternate-family review plus specialist-agent research, leaves two confirmed rollout blockers and eight bounded source-alignment items: implementation_complete is still invalid in the handoff contract, .claude/settings.json is still omitted from the consumer package contract, the managed consumer package surfaces should stay aligned with the local session-contract trigger coverage, governed Claude review still lacks a canonical packet-transport path that avoids ad hoc shell assembly, governed code/doc/config changes still need a hard gate requiring assigned critical specialist auditor review at the end of each change lane, the governance specialist planner/auditor/QA lanes need tracked hldpro-sim-backed personas, registry surfaces, and packet-only IO contracts so downstream repos consume the same governed resources without ad hoc prompting, bidirectional Codex <> Claude routing still needs a machine-readable dispatch-ownership contract that embeds the required wrapper path, packet transport mode, and output artifact contract so validators can reject opposite-family role absorption and ad hoc calls semantically instead of relying on contract-text checks alone, and pinned specialist fallback still needs to be machine-readable so unavailable or exhausted pinned agents can only move upward within their approved family ladder instead of collapsing family ownership.

sprints.name: Packet and review
sprints.goal: Capture the final rollout-blocker set, bounded acceptance criteria, and alternate-family review before implementation-ready promotion.
tasks: Write the issue-587 PDCAR, structured plan, planning execution scope, and planned handoff.
Capture specialist review on whether the blocker set is correctly bounded and still blocks downstream rollout.
Run the governed Claude review path against the packet before implementation.
acceptance_criteria: The issue-587 packet validates locally as planning_only.
The packet names the exact rollout blockers and blocks downstream rollout until they are closed.
Alternate-family review is recorded through the governed wrapper path before implementation-ready promotion.

sprints.name: Rollout blocker hardening
sprints.goal: Land the minimum governance-source fixes that clear the final rollout blocker set and prove them locally.
tasks: Patch the handoff schema, handoff validator, and tests so implementation_complete is a valid lifecycle state.
Patch the governance tooling package and consumer pull-state so .claude/settings.json is part of the managed contract where required.
Keep the hldpro-governance local-CI trigger coverage and the managed consumer package contract aligned, with regression tests proving the package surface remains in lockstep.
Hard-gate scripts/codex-review.sh claude to a file-backed packet transport path so governed review packets are passed by packet file ref and inline shell interpolation fails closed.

Hard-gate bidirectional Codex <> Claude pinned-agent routing so Codex-primary lanes dispatch Claude-owned pinned roles through the governed Claude path, Claude-primary lanes dispatch Codex-owned pinned roles through the governed Codex path, and neither side may absorb the other side's pinned role.
Hard-gate end-of-change critical specialist auditor assignment so every governed code/doc/config change requires a distinct pinned auditor or QA specialist review before merge or closeout.
Define tracked governance specialist planner, auditor, and QA lanes backed by shared hldpro-sim personas, and expose them through registry and consumer-manifest surfaces for downstream repos.
Hard-gate specialist-agent packet contracts so those lanes accept only schema-valid packets, return only structured packet outputs, and declare tracked packet transport plus availability evidence.

Add a machine-readable dispatch-ownership contract to structured plans and handoffs so validators can prove the primary session family, opposite-family owned roles, pinned wrapper path, packet transport, output artifact path, non-absorption semantics, and same-family fallback ladder for unavailable or exhausted pinned specialists.
acceptance_criteria: package-handoff accepts implementation_complete in both schema and validator paths.
Consumer package contract requires .claude/settings.json where the session package requires it.
Session-contract trigger coverage and the managed consumer package surfaces remain aligned in local proof.

Governed Claude review packets are hard-gated to a file-backed packet transport path: scripts/codex-review.sh claude passes a packet file ref through the governed wrapper flow, and inline shell interpolation fails closed.

Bidirectional Codex <> Claude pinned-agent routing is hard-gated in repo-facing contract surfaces: Codex-primary lanes must dispatch Claude-owned pinned roles through the governed Claude path, Claude-primary lanes must dispatch Codex-owned pinned roles through the governed Codex path, and neither side may absorb the other side's pinned role.
Every governed code/doc/config change ends with assigned critical specialist auditor review by a distinct pinned auditor or QA lane before merge or closeout.

Tracked governance specialist planner, auditor, and QA agents are available through shared hldpro-sim personas, AGENT_REGISTRY.md, and docs/hldpro-sim-consumer-pull-state.json for downstream consumer rollout.
Specialist-agent lanes are packet-backed only: validators can prove packet input ref, packet transport, packet output ref, and availability evidence for each declared specialist lane.

Bidirectional Codex <> Claude dispatch ownership is machine-readable in plan and handoff artifacts, including the pinned wrapper path and packet/output contract, and validators reject opposite-family role absorption or ad hoc call paths structurally rather than relying only on required contract strings.

Pinned specialist fallback criteria are machine-readable and hard-gated: unavailable or exhausted pinned agents may fall back only within their owned family, only through the approved quality-upgrade ladder, with required fallback logging and no cross-family role absorption.
The strengthened source checks are covered by regression tests and local proof.

specialist_reviews.focus: Confirm the issue-587 blocker set is still blocking for downstream rollout and is correctly bounded.
specialist_reviews.summary: The issue-587 blocker set is real and source-owned: implementation_complete handoff lifecycle alignment, consumer settings contract coverage, package/trigger alignment, canonical governed Claude packet transport, end-of-change critical specialist auditor assignment, and pinned-specialist fallback policy still need source fixes before downstream rollout resumes. Routing must be hard-gated so Codex-primary lanes dispatch Claude-owned pinned roles through the governed Claude path, Claude-primary lanes dispatch Codex-owned pinned roles through the governed Codex path, and neither side may absorb the other side's pinned role. When a pinned specialist is unavailable or exhausted, fallback must stay inside the owned family and move only through the approved quality ladder.

alternate_model_review.reviewer: Claude specialist review via scripts/codex-review.sh claude
alternate_model_review.summary: Accepted alternate-family packet review: no additional source blocker identified; implementation-ready promotion may proceed after the packet records the actual review artifact path, keeps AC4 machine-testable during implementation, and preserves Claude as alternate-family review only while routing ownership remains hard-gated across Codex <> Claude.

execution_handoff.approved_scope_summary: Issue #587 is approved for implementation-ready work over the remaining governance-source rollout blockers. The accepted review preserves Claude as alternate-family review only while repo-facing routing language is hard-gated: Codex-primary lanes dispatch Claude-owned pinned roles through the governed Claude path, Claude-primary lanes dispatch Codex-owned pinned roles through the governed Codex path, and neither side may absorb the other side's pinned role. Pinned specialist fallback must remain inside the owned family and move only through the approved quality ladder when the primary pinned agent is unavailable or exhausted. This lane also hardens tracked hldpro-sim-backed governance specialist planner/auditor/QA lanes so validators can prove structured packet IO and downstream availability.
execution_handoff.next_execution_step: Implement the bounded source fixes, then run specialist QA, alternate-family verification if needed, and closeout proof.
handoff_acceptance_criteria: The accepted alternate-family review is recorded through the governed Claude wrapper path at the actual review artifact path.

The issue-587 implementation remains inside the bounded source-level schema, validator, consumer-contract, governed-review transport, hard-gated bidirectional Codex <> Claude routing contract, pinned-specialist fallback ladder, and end-of-change specialist-auditor gate: Codex-primary lanes dispatch Claude-owned pinned roles through the governed Claude path, Claude-primary lanes dispatch Codex-owned pinned roles through the governed Codex path, neither side may absorb the other side's pinned role, unavailable or exhausted pinned specialists may fall back only within the owned family and approved quality ladder, and every governed code/doc/config change ends with distinct pinned auditor or QA specialist review before merge or closeout.

Tracked governance specialist planner, auditor, and QA lanes must remain packet-backed and hldpro-sim-backed: each declared specialist lane resolves through AGENT_REGISTRY.md, docs/hldpro-sim-consumer-pull-state.json, file-backed packet input, and structured packet output.
Primary-session dispatch ownership must be machine-readable in the plan/handoff chain so validators can reject opposite-family role absorption without relying on text-only contract checks.
Downstream rollout remains blocked until this final governance-source blocker lane is closed.

material_deviation_rules: Do not resume downstream rollout on the strength of issues #583 and #585 alone.
Do not weaken the issue-587 blocker set into documentation-only warnings.
Do not widen this governance-source lane into direct consumer-repo implementation work.
